paper_title: Extending Thomassen's conjecture to directed graphs
summary: Question 6.2 asks whether every digraph admitting a height function is non-avoidable, motivated by the observation that all digraphs proved non-avoidable in the paper (one-directed complete bipartite graphs and oriented trees) do allow a height function. The paper was posted in October 2025 and no follow-up resolving this question was found in the literature as of May 2026. The question remains open alongside related open problems (Conjecture 6.1 on odd-cycle orientations, Questions 6.3–6.4 on C4 orientations), all part of the broader program of characterizing avoidable digraphs.

notes: No follow-up paper addressing Question 6.2 found after 3 web searches and fetching both the arXiv abstract page and the HTML full text. The question is recent (October 2025) and is explicitly posed as open by the authors. The HTML version of the paper confirms the exact statement and motivation of Question 6.2.
